title: Except The Heaven Had Come So Near,
content:
Except the heaven had come so near,
    So seemed to choose my door,
    The distance would not haunt me so;
    I had not hoped before.

    But just to hear the grace depart
    I never thought to see,
    Afflicts me with a double loss;
    'T is lost, and lost to me.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "Except The Heaven Had Come So Near,", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
